perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise addressed in an Intellectual Property Amendment, VS hereby assigns to PPD (or Sponsor as the case may be) all rights that VS may have in any invention, technology, know-how or other intellectual property which is developed with use of Confidential Information provided to VS by PPD. Additionally, VS shall assist PPD (or Sponsor), at PPD's (or Sponsor's) sole cost and expense, in obtaining or extending protection therefor.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise addressed in an Intellectual Property Amendment, VS hereby retains all rights to any invention, technology, know-how or other intellectual property which is developed with use of Confidential Information provided to VS by PPD. Additionally, VS shall not be obligated to assist PPD (or Sponsor) in obtaining or extending protection therefor, and any such assistance will be subject to a separate agreement and compensation.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding intellectual property rights. The original text clearly assigns rights developed using PPD's confidential information to PPD. The modified text states that VS retains all rights and is not obligated to assist PPD in protecting those rights. This creates uncertainty regarding ownership of intellectual property developed during the alliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Intellectual Property. Nothing contained herein, nor the delivery of any information to either party hereunder, shall be deemed to grant the other party a right or license under any patent or patent application or to any know-how, technology, invention or other intellectual property of the other party or of the Sponsor. All inventions, patents, know-how, trademarks, copyrights, information, data, software, methodologies, writings and other property in any form whatsoever, which are provided to either party and which were owned or controlled by the providing party ("Originator") shall remain the sole property of the Originator. The parties' ownership rights in any inventions, patents, trademarks, copyrights, software, methodologies, writings and other property in any form whatsoever that results out of the development of a Joint Solution shall be addressed by the parties by separate written agreement which may include, an applicable Work Order and/or an amendment to this Agreement ("Intellectual Property Amendment") . Unless otherwise addressed in an Intellectual Property Amendment, VS hereby retains all rights to any invention, technology, know-how or other intellectual property which is developed with use of Confidential Information provided to VS by PPD. Additionally, VS shall not be obligated to assist PPD (or Sponsor) in obtaining or extending protection therefor, and any such assistance will be subject to a separate agreement and compensation. Unless otherwise addressed in an Intellectual Property Amendment, PPD hereby assigns to VS all rights that PPD may have in any invention, technology, know-how or other intellectual property which is developed with use of Confidential Information provided to PPD by VS. Additionally, PPD shall assist VS, at the sole cost and expense of VS, in obtaining or extending protection therefor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement regarding intellectual property rights. The original text assigned rights developed using PPD's confidential information to PPD. The modified text asserts VS retains all rights and is not obligated to assist PPD in protecting them, which creates a clear conflict within the 'Intellectual Property' section, specifically the statements 'Unless otherwise addressed in an Intellectual Property Amendment, VS hereby retains all rights to any invention...Additionally, VS shall not be obligated to assist PPD (or Sponsor) in obtaining or extending protection therefor...Unless otherwise addressed in an Intellectual Property Amendment, PPD hereby assigns to VS all rights that PPD may have in any invention...' This demonstrates conflicting positions on who owns the intellectual property and who is responsible for its protection, causing a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: VS shall submit monthly invoices to PPD detailing its activities and fees in accordance with Section 5 of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, invoices must be submitted to PPD within thirty (30) days of performing the Service or incurring the expense.
perturbation.changed_text: VS shall submit invoices to PPD detailing its activities and fees, but the timing of invoice submission will be determined solely by VS. VS is not obligated to submit invoices within thirty (30) days of performing the Service or incurring the expense.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original requirement for VS to submit invoices monthly and within 30 days of service. The revised text gives VS complete discretion over invoice timing, creating a contradiction that could lead to disputes over payment schedules and financial obligations. The removal of the 30 day deadline creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VS shall submit invoices to PPD detailing its activities and fees, but the timing of invoice submission will be determined solely by VS. VS is not obligated to submit invoices within thirty (30) days of performing the Service or incurring the expense. PPD shall have no obligation to issue payment to VS for Services performed or expenses incurred which were not invoiced to PPD in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "VS is not obligated to submit invoices within thirty (30) days of performing the Service or incurring the expense." The contradicted text then states "PPD shall have no obligation to issue payment to VS for Services performed or expenses incurred which were not invoiced to PPD in accordance with this Agreement." The agreement now has contradictory terms about when invoices must be submitted, and the effects of not invoicing in accordance to it. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties. This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
